name: Quote cart as the conversion event
logic: In B2B, price is negotiated and the order is a quote, not a checkout — so forcing 'add to cart → pay' kills intent. The right conversion event is a multi-line RFQ basket: the buyer assembles exactly what they want, with full spec context, and asks for a quote. You capture a qualified lead that carries its own requirements, instead of a contact-form 'please call me'. Gartner shows B2B buyers run most of the journey rep-free and spend only ~17% of their time with suppliers — so meet them where they are and let them self-assemble the ask.

evidence.title: Gartner — 61% of B2B Buyers Prefer a Rep-Free Buying Experience
evidence.note: Gartner newsroom, 2025-06-25. URL resolves; direct fetch is bot-blocked (HTTP 403) so the 61% figure is per Gartner's own release title, not independently re-read.

evidence.title: Gartner — The B2B Buying Journey
evidence.note: Buyers spend only ~17% of buying time with any one supplier — capture intent without forcing a rep call or a card payment.

exemplars.name: Misumi
exemplars.why: Configure-and-quote B2B flow at scale — the cart is a specification, not a checkout.

exemplars.name: Grainger
exemplars.why: RFQ / quote tooling built directly into the buying flow.

metricsNote: B2B buyers spend only ~17% of buying time with suppliers; a majority prefer a rep-free experience (Gartner).
